title: Paper 3 — The D4/J3 Triality Surface, the Correction Surface, and the Arf Edge Glue
abstract: This paper unifies two foundational threads: the correction surface of the Rule 30 ANF decomposition (F2 quadratic form, Arf invariant, edge-glue criterion) and the D4/J3 triality surface (multi-language registration theorem linking LCR states, D4 axis/sheet codes, and diagonal J3(O) carriers). The correction term defines a finite F2 quadratic form with Arf invariant 0 (hyperbolic). The hyperbolic structure is the algebraic reason the correction can be glued along shared boundaries. Independently, the eight LCR states admit a bijective D4-style axis/sheet chart, a diagonal J3(O) carrier with trace preservation, and trace-2 diagonal idempotents for the shell-2 states. The D4 axis/sheet codec, D12 action envelope, J3(O) atlas, S3 Weyl orbit, F4 action, triality automorphism of D4, and Freudenthal–Tits magic square are the exceptional-algebraic extensions. All 7 affirmative claims are closed by executable receipts.

D_claims.evidence: Theorem 3.1 — direct evaluation of correction(C,R) = C·¬R

D_claims.claim: F2 quadratic form Q(L,C,R) = C + CR with associated bilinear form B_Q(u,v) = C₁R₂ + C₂R₁
Rank of Q is 2 over GF(2)

D_claims.evidence: Corollary 4.3 — direct evaluation on all 8 states
Theorem 5.1 — explicit truth-table evaluation over all 8 states; sum = 2 ≡ 0 (mod 2)

D_claims.claim: Arf-matching edge glue criterion: two charts can be glued iff Arf invariants match on shared boundary
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 6.1 — standard quadratic form gluing (Milnor & Husemoller 1973)

D_claims.claim: Correction is gluing-compatible (hyperbolic → matches any hyperbolic form)
Independence of correction from L: ∂correction/∂L = 0 over GF(2)
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 7.1 — formula depends only on C and R

D_claims.claim: D4 axis/sheet codec partitions 8 LCR states into 4 axis classes of size 2
D12 group of order 12 acts on the D4 axis/sheet codec preserving structure
J3(O) trace-2 idempotents are E₁₁+E₂₂, E₁₁+E₃₃, E₂₂+E₃₃
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 10.2 — direct verification of trace and idempotency

D_claims.claim: S3 Weyl orbit on 3 trace-2 idempotents is the Weyl group of A₂ = SU(3)
3 trace-2 idempotents in bijection with 3 shell-2 LCR states
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 11.3 — both sets cardinality 3 under same S3 action

D_claims.claim: F4 is Aut(J3(O)) and contains SU(3) as maximal subgroup
Triality automorphism of D4 permutes three 8-dimensional SO(8) representations
Freudenthal–Tits magic square contains F4, E6, E7, E8
Local triality surface τ(L,C,R) = (axis, sheet, φ) is a faithful three-language presentation
D_claims.evidence: Theorem 15.1 — bijection + trace preservation + idempotency proofs

I_claims.claim: Naming of the correction surface S_correction
I_claims.reasoning: Structural reading; the mathematical object is D but the naming is author's framing

I_claims.claim: Edge glue criterion as structural criterion for FLCR boundary repair
I_claims.reasoning: Arf match is standard math; application to FLCR boundary repair is author's framing

I_claims.claim: Hyperbolic descriptor and gluing compatibility consequence
I_claims.reasoning: Structural reading of standard quadratic form classification

I_claims.claim: FLCR lattice ladder (LCR → D4 → J3(O) → D12 → F4 → E8 → Leech)
I_claims.reasoning: Author's structural framing of the paper series path
The bijection is D; the SM identification is interpretive mapping

I_claims.claim: Magic square as the deep structure of the FLCR series
I_claims.reasoning: The magic square is D; the framing as deep structure is I

I_claims.claim: F4 → Niemeier route as conjectural path

I_claims.reasoning: D12 itself is D; the chart-level descriptor is author's framing

I_claims.claim: Cayley–Dickson extension of the LCR kernel
I_claims.reasoning: Cayley–Dickson construction is D; extension to LCR kernel is I

I_claims.claim: Robot-arm calibration as non-toy domain example
I_claims.reasoning: Pedagogical analogy, not a physical experiment
